key_metrics: Premium revenue of $30.71 billion, up 9.9% YoY
Benefits expense of $27.99 billion, up 11.4% YoY
Diluted earnings per common share of $1.62, down from $3.98 in Q3 2024
qoq_changes: Implied sequential net income plummeted, with Q3 contributing only $195 million to the $1.98 billion year-to-date total
Recognized a new $63 million loss on sale of business during the quarter
Operating income compressed significantly, dropping to $400 million for the quarter
new_risks: Medical cost trend acceleration, evidenced by benefits expense (+11.4%) growing faster than premium revenue (+9.9%)
Severe margin deterioration with operating income falling 45% YoY
Underlying profitability weakness masked by an $86 million income tax benefit in the quarter
management_tone: Cautious, as strong top-line premium growth is being severely undermined by accelerating medical benefit expenses and rising operating costs.
analyst_note: Humana's Q3 2025 results reveal significant margin compression, with a 59% YoY drop in net income despite 11% revenue growth. Accelerating medical costs, rising operating expenses, and a $63 million divestiture loss highlight near-term operational headwinds that management must address to restore baseline profitability.
